mapping.a_element: noise bias (dephasing dominating other noise types)
mapping.b_element: probability bias (p_c representing the dominance of ferromagnetic bonds over antiferromagnetic ones)

mapping.a_element: accuracy threshold for fault-tolerant quantum computation
mapping.b_element: multicritical point location (p_c) in spin glasses

brief_justification: While both papers exploit asymmetry in their respective systems (noise bias in quantum computation vs. bond probability bias in spin glasses), the underlying mechanisms differ fundamentally: Paper A relies on quantum error correction and threshold theorems for biased noise, whereas Paper B uses duality and partition function relationships in classical statistical mechanics to locate multicritical points. The core machinery is not transferable.
